plan_metadata.name: DA Add Action Import Existing API

steps.description: Click the "Declarative Agent" option in the "New Project" modal of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "No Action" option from the dropdown menu under "Add an action to your declarative agent" in the "Create Declarative Agent" window of Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit.
Click the "Start with a New API" option in the "Create an Action" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "None" option in the "Select an authentication type" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "TypeScript" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown menu at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.
Click the "Default folder" option in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select '/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects' as the project location in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "appPackage" folder in the VS Code Explorer sidebar to expand its contents.
Click the "apiSpecificationFile" item under the "appPackage" folder in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "repair.yaml" file under the "apiSpecificationFile" folder in the VS Code Explorer sidebar to open it.

Click on the "url" value field in the repair.yml file within Visual Studio Code, specifically at the end of the line containing "${[OPENAPI_SERVER_URL]}/api", to position the text cursor for editing the API server URL.

Right-click on the "url" line in the repair.yml file within the apiSpecificationFile folder in Visual Studio Code, targeting the path definition under the servers section for the Repair Service API configuration.

Right-click the context menu and select the "Cut" option in Visual Studio Code to remove the selected text from the repair.yml file. The click targets the "Cut" menu item highlighted in blue within the editor's context menu.
Press the Enter key while editing the "repair.yml" file in the VS Code editor within the "apiSpecificationFile" folder.
Press the Up Arrow key to move the cursor up one line in the repair.yml file within the VS Code editor.
Press the Tab key to indent the selected line in the "repair.yml" file within Visual Studio Code's code editor.
Type the text '- url: https://pluginrentu.azurewebsites.net/api' into the repair.yml file within the Visual Studio Code editor, specifically at line 7 under the 'servers' section.
Save changes to the "repair.yml" file in Visual Studio Code by pressing Ctrl+S.

Right-click on the highlighted URL (`https://pluginrentu.azurewebsites.net/api`) in the `repair.yml` file within Visual Studio Code, under the `apiSpecificationFile` directory, to access context menu options related to the API server endpoint.
Click the "Close" option in the editor tab context menu for the file "repair.yml" in Visual Studio Code's workspace explorer.
Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" icon in the Visual Studio Code sidebar to open the toolkit panel.

Click the "Create New Agent/App" button in the DEVELOPMENT section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit pane in Visual Studio Code, triggering the tooltip "Create a new agent/app from scratch or begin with a sample app."
Click the "Declarative Agent" option in the "New Project" dialog of Visual Studio Code, under the "Agents for Microsoft 365 Copilot" category.
Select the "No Action" option in the "Create Declarative Agent" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents extension panel in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Start with a New API" option in the "Create an Action" dropdown menu within the Visual Studio Code extension interface.
Click the "Enter OpenAPI Description Document Location or Open File..." option in the "OpenAPI Spec Document" dialog within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Enter OpenAPI Description Document Location" input field in the OpenAPI Description Document pop-up at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Open" button at the bottom-right of the file chooser dialog to confirm file selection.
Click the "Toggle all checkboxes" button in the "Select Operation(s) Copilot Can Interact with" dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the blue "plus" button next to "1 Selected" in the "Select Operation(s) Copilot Can Interact with" dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface to add or select an operation.
Click the "Default folder /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects" option in the Workspace Folder selection dialog in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Input an application name" text field in the popup dialog within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Enter 'vscuse-import-api' into the "Application Name" input field in Visual Studio Code's Microsoft 365 Agents extension dialog.
Press Enter to confirm the "vscuse-import-api" input in the "Application Name" dialog box in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Preview README.md" tab in Visual Studio Code to activate the file preview.
Click the "Provision" option under the "LIFECYCLE" section in the MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT sidebar in Visual Studio Code.
Click the red notifications icon in the lower-right corner of the Visual Studio Code window to open the notifications panel.
@assertion there's "provision stage executed successfully" exist.
